claim_boundary: TICKET118 is a preregistered finite holdout. A pass does not prove an eventual bound or the Twin Prime Conjecture; a failure does not disprove Twin Prime and only refutes finite closure of the frozen rule at 8M.

preregistration.holdout_scope: This scale appeared in earlier PrimeProject tracks, but the canonical adjacent-dyadic-pair endpoint statistic has not been evaluated at this horizon.

partition_rule.shells: List every nonempty truncated dyadic outer-divisor shell intersecting [U+1, floor((X+2)/(V+1))] in increasing shell order.
partition_rule.groups: Pair shell indices (0,1), (2,3), and so on; if the shell count is odd, retain the final shell as a singleton.
partition_rule.denominator_policy: Use the same partition for every right Farey denominator q=2,...,32.
partition_rule.optimization_ban: Do not search partitions, signs, subsets, weights, exponents, or denominator-specific groupings after reading the 8M endpoint coefficients.

primary_endpoint.failure_condition: less than or equal to zero
primary_endpoint.meaning: A pass is one post-registration finite closure only. A failure refutes finite closure of this frozen rule at 8M. Neither outcome proves or disproves the Twin Prime Conjecture.

secondary_endpoints: canonical numerator budget divided by the fully signed numerator budget
canonical partition compared with the best width-two contiguous partition, reported descriptively without changing the primary endpoint
time-domain, endpoint-profile, phase, and real-Gram reconstruction errors
preregistration.claim_boundary: No 8M result was used to choose this rule or endpoint. The registration does not assert that the primary endpoint will pass, that the rule is eventually stable, or that any of the four conjectures is proved.

primary_result.interpretation: This is exactly one post-registration finite test. It is not an all-sufficiently-large-X theorem.

twin_canonical_adjacent_pair_holdout.proof_boundary: TICKET118 is a preregistered finite holdout. A pass does not prove an eventual bound or the Twin Prime Conjecture; a failure does not disprove Twin Prime and only refutes finite closure of the frozen rule at 8M.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: preserve the independent target during the Twin preregistered 8M holdout
attempts.attempt: Preserve the existing infinite target; no Twin canonical-pair holdout result is transferred.
attempts.obstruction: TICKET118 supplies no new target-specific infinite theorem for this problem.
attempts.next_experiment: Continue NonCircularExplicitFormulaKernelPositivity with its own proof and counterexample oracle.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: preregistered post-selection holdout with frozen canonical grouping and primary endpoint
attempts.attempt: Freeze the canonical adjacent-shell pairing and positive finite lower expression before computing its 8M endpoint coefficients, then report the result without retuning.
attempts.obstruction: One preregistered 8M closure does not prove eventual closure, a uniform bilinear estimate, or positivity of the comparison term on all sufficiently large scales.
attempts.next_experiment: Expand each canonical factor-four outer-divisor group into its signed Mobius bilinear form and prove a denominator-summed uniform bound, or find an unbounded Vaughan-realizable failure sequence.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified last-twin counterexample.
